data.title: Vixen by Micheline Pitt launches Bride of Frankenstein Collection to mark 90th anniversary. Celebrating horror icon through modern fashion.
data.description: Los Angeles-based fashion brand Vixen by Micheline Pitt, founded by a female entrepreneur, recently launched the official Bride of Frankenstein capsule collection. This collection pays homage to the iconic horror figure with a modern and feminine touch. The launch took place on July 25, 2025, in collaboration with Universal Products & Experiences. This exciting collection is set to captivate fashion enthusiasts with its unique and innovative take on the horror genre.

data.title: Gold prices drop over 1%; Phillips 66 reports upbeat earnings. Dow Jones up 0.19% to 44,779.21, NASDAQ up 0.37% to 21,135.49, S&P 500 rose 0.32% to 6,383.53. Phillips 66 beats market estimates with earnings per share of $2.38 and $33.52 billion in sales. Linkhome Holdings Inc. shares surge 67% to $9.42.
data.description: Stocks in the U.S. saw an increase in trading, with the Dow Jones rising by 0.2% to 44,779.21, the NASDAQ up by 0.37% to 21,135.49, and the S&P 500 gaining 0.32% to reach 6,383.53 on Friday. Financial sectors showed a significant jump of 0.5%, whereas real estate stocks experienced a decline of 0.8% in trading on Thursday. Phillips 66 (NYSE: PSX) surpassed expectations in the second quarter with adjusted earnings per share of $2.38, beating estimates of $1.69 per share, with quarterly sales reaching $33.52 billion compared to anticipated $32.53 billion. Linkhome Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: LHAI) saw a notable rise of 67% to $9.42 following an upsized announcement. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Polymarket traders bet $2M on Ghislaine Maxwell outcomes after secret DOJ meeting and congressional subpoena.
data.description: Activity on the cryptocurrency-powered prediction platform Polymarket has experienced a significant increase following an unexpected development involving Ghislaine Maxwell. The former associate of Jeffrey Epstein had a meeting with U.S. Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche on July 24, leading to a surge in speculation and bets on various Epstein-related scenarios. Maxwell's lawyer, David Oscar Markus, stated that the meeting was "very productive," highlighting her full cooperation and lack of legal privilege assertions during questioning. Subsequently, a House committee issued a subpoena on the same day, compelling Maxwell to testify publicly on August 11, which indicates a notable shift in the public legal actions concerning the Epstein case. Consequently, traders on Polymarket are actively betting on outcomes related to the Epstein case. One of the most popular markets on the platform is "Will Ghislaine Maxwell testify before Congress in 2025?" with a high level of activity.
